reviews.review_msg: Professor Fleming is an outstanding professor. He is thoughtful and facilitates excellent group discussions that were productive for the class despite the difficult and abstract fourth year content. In terms of grading criteria, he is a tough essay marker but a generous test administrator with clear expectations. Overall, challenging but good!

Easy course, only showed up to class a few times and finished with an A-. Not a fan of literature but the novels were actually very interesting. Professor Fleming gave VERY clear criteria for assignments so you knew exactly what you needed to do. He even provided us with all of the questions for the final so it really just depends on your TA.

I am not that into literature to begin with so I never really like any English classes. That being said, I did find Prof Fleming to be a pretty good lecturer. I was bored a lot.The term paper was graded tough but the exam was ok. If you have questions, you need to ask your ta as the prof does not help.
Awful class, the professor did not help at all.He expected too much for a first year class.

Jim Fleming: no-nonsense, no-faker, old school admirable honesty of scholarship! Ideologically & philosophically, I could not be more apart from this guy, but you take a course with Prof. Fleming and you get your faith in decency of high academics restored!

JD is a caring and passionate prof. Course concepts are often very philosophical and may be difficult to follow, so I would suggest writing down everything he says verbatimit'll help you in the long run. His course definitely makes you work hard and think deeply about course concepts which I found really interesting. Would take again!

Very lecture heavy and not so clear. The upside is that his courses seemed to be organized for online learning, unlike other profs but I wouldn't take his class again. I wouldn't have had any idea where to start if it hadn't been for my TA. Flemming expects a lot from first year students, if you're an english major you might enjoy it

I would not recommend taking this class! Professor Fleming talks about large and complex ideas but makes his analysis very broad and vague to the point where it is not helpful at all. This is a first year English course and its as if he expects students to already be amazing literary analysts. He preaches that his students should be confident in
Professor Fleming is a quality professor. He is witty and caring. His lectures are insightful and engaging. I would recommend him as a professor because he is very organized with remote schooling.

Taken during COVID so it was online. JD is a very caring prof and definitely loves the subject of literary criticisms. It reminded me more of a philosophy class, although it was still interesting. He likes asking big questions even if he himself can't answer them during tutorials, which gives students a lot to think about for future discussions.

Papers depend more on your TA than anything else but final was easy. Just write down what he says word for word (no point form, verbatim) and regurgitate it on the paper. There's easier W credits but if you're interested in the subject matter it's not bad.

This prof is a nice man who is very passionate about his work, but he is very tough grader. He doesn't give clear criteria for anything and then marks super hard because you didn't write the way he wanted you to. I spent a lot of time on this class and put in a lot of effort and am not expecting a grade that I would like. I'd skip this prof.
JD is a good lecturer, but a tough marker. Be prepared to work hard and still not get the grade you wanted.

JD is a great prof! I enjoy his lecture style and the way he makes the material engaging. His classes are lecture heavy, but it is always reasonable, just come ready to listen and write! I would recommend anyone to take a class with JD, he makes English awesome!

JD is a great prof! I really enjoy his lecture style (however, he doesn't post slides or anything it's just writing what he says mostly). He makes the literature engaging and his criteria are clear and concise. I would highly recommend taking a class with him!

He definitely has his own style of teaching: lecture heavy, non discussion/group project oriented. Though he is open to questions during class, the nature of his courses are mostly taking notes from what he says during lectures. So if you like this style of teaching, then you are in for a good time. Also his grading criteria are very clear.
Great prof, energetic and articulate, breaks things down well and has clear and fair grading criteria.

Professor James Fleming is without a doubt one of the best English professor I have ever know. I am glad to be one of his students, He is a real character, funny and down to earth. well respected and does anything to help students to achieve their grades. Great instructor, father and outstanding person.

Not a class I would take or recommend, was very vague when asked for help. The content was interesting but the way it was delivered was poorly done. He shot down student answers and questions at the beginning of the semester that many stopped participating and the class was 3 hours of him talking. Didn't give clear instructions on assignments.

Lectures are theoretical and challenging, but if you do the readings and are interested in the subject, his classes are enriching. Prefers close reading to research. Take copious notes and show up. Found him somewhat curt one-on-one but not openly rude. Would rather email than talk on office hours. Expectations for assignments, final are clear.

Best lecturer I've had the pleasure of listening to in my 4 year ENGL major. Toughest marker I've had in my 4 year ENGL major.   Pay attention to his lectures, it shouldn't be difficult. Don't try to push the envelope, stick to what you're taught and you'll do fine. Attendance is mandatory (if you want a decent grade) but enjoyable.

An excellent professor. Probably my favourite professor up till now and I am not even an English major. He inspires you to read all the novels. Great guy, and great lecturer. I definitely recommend taking his class. Has a very Fair final exam!!

I can't say much for his grading as I had a TA mark my work, but his lectures are  fantastic. The final exam was very fair and if you paid attention in lecture (or listened to them online), attended tutorials, and took good notes your should be fine. He has a great late paper policy, though watch out for response papers:  due dates sneak up you!
Awesome proof, explains concepts very well!

Hits all of the notes well in lecture. He sometimes got hung up on a certain poem or excerpt for too long and had to rush the last bit of the lecture but that's fine. One of the only people who I've heard read poetry and not make me cringe. The final is fine if you attend lecture regularly and go over the exam review sheets.

One of the best professors I've ever had, his enthusiasm for the subject is so infectious and his lectures are golden. Professor Fleming is super funny, and nice to everyone. I wouldn't say this class was easy, but I only went to half the lectures and I still did ok, mostly because of his great review sheets. Most of the texts are public domain.

Every lecture was like a well-written, award-winning essay. He's frighteningly intelligent.  But final exam is just his lecture material. Basically regurgitate what he has said throughout the semester. Can't talk about his marking, because I had a TA mark my essays, but I once saw him  strike an entire page of an essay with his red pen :S
He is a good lecturer and prof but the material was just too difficult for me. Make sure you study his lectures and read the material, the final is based on his lectures. Marks hard on papers...

Fantastic, engaging prof with clear and accurate interpretations of material. Early Modern texts are challenging- he turns them into a cakewalk. Though the subject material becomes easier to digest and write about, your material will be critically evaluated (so, write well). Like a lot of eng. profs: he's crazy but that's good.
He's awesome!

Great lecturer, his lectures are very interesting does a great job of keeping students engaged. Final was pretty straightforward, I thought his grading scheme was very fair. Probably my favourite professor at SFU!

Engaging, knowledgeable, and quick. A smart guy who encourages interesting class discussion, but is stuck in his own interpretations. Willing to listen to alternatives but not consider them seriously. Fairly arrogant and cold in office hours. Gave good essay critiques and long, complex lectures. I learned a lot but didn't feel welcome in his class.

Do not take this class with this prof...all he does it talk about what he thinks a certain poem means...you will walk out of this course learning nothing but "flemings interpretations of poems"....i studied very hard in this class but still only ended up with a B+....plus the final is murder !!!

Very interesting lectures! I'm not a terribly big fan of poetry but his lectures were insightful and informative. The assignments were reasonable. If you need a W credit then this class is highly recommended.
ABSOLUTELY AMAZING! I didn't want to miss a single lecture, he was sooo captivating.

Pretty good prof. but depends on how your TA is really... Make sure you take really good notes of lectures because it will be VERY useful for the final. He'll give you a study guide and just study whatever is on there. He gives one essay and has an early due date(basically a chance for you to revise your paper if you don't like your mark)
no coment

This prof is amazing! Energetic, enthusiastic and extremely intelligent and interested in his subject. The lectures are interesting, and the textbook covers a wide range of subject matter. However, I wouldn't recommend this class if you have no interest in poetry whatsoever. With some interest, the class becomes both challenging and fun! Love it.

Prof is SUPER nice & enthusiastic. Go to lectures, they are really helpful and his final is based on alot of the things he talked about. Didn't get the grade I want but that's because of TA. He will give you an entire outline of his final and only things on the outline will be tested.
I didn't get the grade I wanted, but this prof is something else. He taught everything with conviction and that impressed me a lot. Don't expect easy work from him, but his class is a blessing.
Great teacher, just be prepared to back up your statements or presentations in class 'cause he'll cut through the BS.

I really enjoyed his class.  he is extremely well-spoken and is very open to having students drop by to discuss anything from the class, to paper topics, etc.  He is also very generous by offering a free revision of papers (if you can get them in by the deadline).  Overall, a really great instructor!

The problem with the faculty is that there are a million kids who want a degree so they can be Elementary teachers. If you're one of those people, don't take this class, as Fleming is passionate about examining LITERATURE (Milton, in this case), and you will be smoked if you bring your ditzy 'I'm participating by repeating what he just said' junk.
Professor Fleming encourages intelligent discussion. He is interested, and knows his subject material well. If anybody knows Milton, it's this guy.
has an inverted, paradoxical logic. extremely precise assignment guidelines. rude commentary on papers. egotistical and patronizing.

This man is quite crazy, he goes off on tangents about the strangest things. The class is pretty good, the short papers are not bad so make the most of your marks there. The term paper is 8 pages so really try to start early on that one. Theres no midterm so thats always good. And make sure to take notes about what he thinks about poems.

The best word to describe him is arrogant.  His assignments were not clear although he rambled on about them for hours and he has favorites in the class.  He was very disorganized and the lectures were uninspiring.  His comments on papers were cruel.  For one girl he wrote "I don't even know why you're an English major".

V. interesting lectures, but can be a bit abstract at times.  Course wasn't too difficult but the term paper was v. long.  Believes his view on a poem is the only/correct one...can be a bit daunting, but, all in all, wasn't too bad.
Awesome lectures.  Really helps if you're at the lectures, they're entertaining and interesting.  And it helps with poem reference for the final.
He is a GREAT teacher, he always makes lectures interesting and you can tell he really enjoys poetry! I actually enjoyed going to lecture, and he isn't too bad  to look at either :)

1st year, 1st sem, great class to take!  I loved it, it was a lot of fun.  James is an amazing prof who captivates you right from the start.  Towards midterms lectures get kind of dull, but that is only because the course content changes from context to form so it's not his fault.  He tries his best to make his lectures as entertaining as possible.

Good prof with interesting views. Drills his take on things into you. Must attend lectures for notes given that he doesn't not have handouts/ taped lectures available online. Lectures are not deadly, but can becoming quite boring with the repetition and many examples he gives.
Probably my favourite prof-he's funny,clear, and really knowledgable.. and hes a HOTTIE so lecture is never boring :)
who doesn't want to learn from someone who has something to offer? fleming definitely knows his material. a bit intimidating, but easy to listen to as his fondness of himself is quite amusing.

You must attend lectures if you want to understand his take on all the texts...you can't rely on someone's notes for this.  He goes on some tangents, but they're usually interesting ones.  His essay questions and test questions are a little difficult, but if you study hard, you'll do well.  Make sure to attend tutorials, attendance is taken.

I appreciated his comments in his marking, but as a lecturer he is unclear, verbose, redundant, aloof. The course itself was poorly structured (seen in its multiple revisions). Difficult to get a clear response from him. Interpersonal and educator skills need work.
Very smart, funny, and nice professor.  His teaching material and method can be a bit intimidating at first, but he's a very fair marker.  This was a very interesting class.

He really knows his stuff and he speaks at a really high level which was somewhat daunting at first but I soon appreciated it as it certainly improved my vocab.  He wasn't the easiest essay marker but gave us vary good study tips for the final.

Can you say 'Kevin Spacey'?  Even if he couldn't teach, his looks are sufficient to get him by in life.  A brilliant professor and lecturer...I didn't even have to take notes!  Just wish classes started a bit later in the day.
Easy interesting lectures .... 1 or 2 chapters a week for reading. 1 writing assignment before the final essay. Final was pretty straightforward w/ essay teh end

I took poetry because I did not want to read 5 novels in a semester, I don't even like poetry! But he was an AWESOME prof, made lectures interessting, made me want to come to class - I now have a new appreciation for poetry. Not much work, pretty much a "cupcake class" :P I recommend it!
Good prof but if you are looking to take this because you heard this was an easy course.  Move on.
like some else wrote here, I'm still waiting for a good lecture on Milton. Its his thing, right? And enough with the politics. I signed up for literature.     Does appear fond of himself.

Acted way too intellectual. You often become lost as he swings past things he expects you should know and understand without explaining them. However, definately not an eye sore. And his exam format is good.
Captivating, inspiring, and excellent at communicating ideas!
He's extremely interesting!

He expects a lot from his students, and I congratulate him for his optimism. Honestly though, at times it feels like he thinks he's lecturing a 300 level class, but 204 and 205 ARE designed to "make or break" English students. He's also VERY VER
He is very intelligent, but sometimes has trouble explaining things. Often assumes students know things they don't. An enthusiastic lecturer who made the content interesting!

too intellectual. he has to realize that we're not all at the same level as him and try and use SIMPLER words. i found it was really hard to understand him. also the material was BORING if i knew it would be like this, i never would have taken this course
knows what he's talking about and isn't easy... makes you explain what you say, imagine that.
Prof. Fleming entertained us with a spirited take on difficult material.  But unlike Budra, he didn't let the conversation become mindless.  A picky marker is a good thing, by the way.
He's a smart guy and knows his material. However, he can be a jerk and is a picky marker.

I got an A in his course, but I still didn't like him very much -- I thought he was kind of stuck up and disorganized.
 Also, he's supposed to be a big expert on Milton, and I was looking forward to his lecture on Paradise lost but, he got a TA to substit
very entertaining, but difficult material
No Comments
very entertaining lectures, but bad material
knows what he's talking about. very funny in tutorials. great teacher
No Comments
No Comments
